content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of integers of length NNN. Find the sum of difference between maximum and minimum elements of all sub-arrays. Formally, find the value of the following expression :
∑i=1N∑j=iNmax(Ai,Ai+1...Aj)−min(Ai,Ai+1...Aj)\sum\limits_{i=1}^{N}\sum\limits_{j=i}^{N} max(A_i, A_{i + 1} . . . A_j) - min(A_i, A_{i + 1} . . . A_j)i=1∑N​j=i∑N​max(Ai​,Ai+1​...Aj​)−min(Ai​,Ai+1​...Aj​)


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each testcase contains a single integer NNN.
Second line of each testcase contains of NNN integers : A1,A2...ANA_1, A_2 . . . A_NA1​,A2​...AN​.


Output Format
For each testcase, output a single integer equal to the value of given expression.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2*10^6.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the sum of (maximum – minimum) over all sub‑arrays of the given array.

solution.approach: For each element we count how many sub‑arrays have it as the maximum and how many have it as the minimum. Using a monotonic stack we can find, for every position i, the distance to the previous greater element (strict) and the next greater‑or‑equal element. The product of these two distances gives the number of sub‑arrays where A[i] is the maximum. The same is done for minima using previous smaller (strict) and next smaller‑or‑equal elements. The answer is Σ A[i] * (cntMax[i] – cntMin[i]). All operations are O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (overall O(total N)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the stacks and auxiliary arrays.
